heading: Sixto exact GeoGebra scene now includes timing-graph curves and passes family [56] validation
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, sixto, geogebra, timing-graph, exact-scene]
body: Rebuilt `sixto_geogebra_extended_scene_export_v1.json`, `sixto_geogebra_extended_adapter_cert.json`, `sixto_geogebra_extended_summary.json`, and `build_sixto_geogebra_graph_extension.py` so the Sixto top-right timing graph is no longer represented only by frame-guide triangles. The raw exact scene now has 22 objects total: 4 sampled timing-graph curve polylines (`curve_graph_light_green`, `curve_graph_green`, `curve_graph_cyan`, `curve_graph_blue`), 3 red reference segments (`seg_graph_v0`, `seg_graph_vplus`, `seg_graph_vminus`), 8 exact curve-derived chord triangles, the 4 prior graph-guide triangles, and the 3 prior stage-angle triangles. The certified family [56] layer still parses only triangle objects, but it now validates 15 exact triangles with all five gates PASS, so the certificate is anchored in the curves themselves instead of only the graph frame. The graph schedule anchor remains stable: relative major X guides `[0.0,189.0,378.0,793.5]`, `x2_vs_geometric_mid = -9.26154`, and `x2_vs_arithmetic_mid = -113.25`. Honest verdict: the Sixto timing graph is now machine-readable on the exact QA substrate as actual curve objects plus certified triangle derivatives; only full analytic curve-law recovery remains open.
